description:
You are a web-developer who has just been handed a large Apache HTTP access-log that lives in

    /home/user/project/logs/access.log

Your goal is to pull out three very specific, regex-driven reports and place them—exactly as described below—inside a **new** directory called

    /home/user/project/reports

(If the directory does not exist, create it.)

Report #1 — “404_summary.log”  
• File path: /home/user/project/reports/404_summary.log  
• One line per **distinct client IP** that triggered an HTTP 404.  
• Line format (single space as delimiter, no tabs):  
      <ip-address> <total-404-count>  
• Sort order:  
  1. Descending on <total-404-count> (numerical)  
  2. If counts are equal, ascending on <ip-address> (lexicographically).  
• The file must end with exactly one trailing newline and contain **no header row** and **no blank lines**.

Report #2 — “500_top_uris.log”  
• File path: /home/user/project/reports/500_top_uris.log  
• One line per requested URI that produced an HTTP 500.  
• You only need the **top five** URIs by frequency.  
• Line format (single space delimiter):  
      <request-uri> <total-500-count>  
• Sort order:  
  1. Descending on <total-500-count> (numerical)  
  2. If counts are equal, ascending on <request-uri> (lexicographically).  
• Exactly five lines, exactly one trailing newline, no header row, no blank lines.

Report #3 — “301_total.txt”  
• File path: /home/user/project/reports/301_total.txt  
• Contains **only one line** with the integer total of all HTTP 301 (“Moved Permanently”) responses in the access log.  
• No extra whitespace, exactly one trailing newline.

Important implementation details the automated tests will enforce:

1. Use regular-expression based filters (e.g., grep -E, awk, sed or similar) and standard UNIX tools; no need for external programming languages.  
2. The three files named above must be the only new files inside /home/user/project/reports.  
3. All three files must be world-readable (chmod 644 or more open).  
4. Do **not** alter /home/user/project/logs/access.log itself.  
5. When you are finished, the command

       ls -l /home/user/project/reports

   should list exactly the three files described, and

       cat <file>

   should show the precise contents and ordering rules laid out above.

Notes & Hints for the exercise (not checked by the grader, but helpful):

• In a default combined Apache log line, the **status code** is the ninth space-separated field.  
• To isolate just the request URI (without HTTP version), you can grab the stuff between the first double-quote after the method and the space before “HTTP/”.  
• Consider piping through sort | uniq -c | sort -nr … to obtain counts.  
• Remember to strip leading spaces that uniq –c introduces if you use that approach.

The grader will open each of the three result files and compare them byte-for-byte to its expected answer, so mind spacing, ordering and newlines carefully.
truth:
Initial environment (before the agent starts):

1. Pre-existing directory structure
   /home/user/
       project/
           logs/
               access.log          (644, normal text file)

2. Exact contents of /home/user/project/logs/access.log:

127.0.0.1 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:55:36 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1043 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
192.168.1.10 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:56:01 +0000] "GET /nonexistent.html HTTP/1.1" 404 512 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
172.16.0.2 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:56:05 +0000] "GET /missing.png HTTP/1.1" 404 512 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
10.0.0.5 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:56:10 +0000] "POST /api/v1/users HTTP/1.1" 500 2560 "-" "curl/7.68.0"
192.168.1.10 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:56:21 +0000] "GET /typo.html HTTP/1.1" 404 512 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
10.0.0.5 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:56:23 +0000] "GET /server-error HTTP/1.1" 500 2048 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
172.16.0.2 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:56:30 +0000] "GET /another-missing.html HTTP/1.1" 404 512 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
203.0.113.9 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:56:40 +0000] "GET /redirect HTTP/1.1" 301 234 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
192.168.1.10 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:57:01 +0000] "GET /old-page HTTP/1.1" 301 234 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
10.0.0.5 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:57:10 +0000] "GET /server-error HTTP/1.1" 500 2048 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
192.168.1.10 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:57:21 +0000] "GET /contact.html HTTP/1.1" 200 1345 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
10.0.0.5 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:57:44 +0000] "POST /api/v1/users HTTP/1.1" 500 2560 "-" "curl/7.68.0"
192.0.2.44 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:58:00 +0000] "GET /server-error HTTP/1.1" 500 2048 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
10.0.0.5 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:58:10 +0000] "GET /server-error HTTP/1.1" 500 2048 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
172.16.0.2 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:58:11 +0000] "GET /redirect HTTP/1.1" 301 234 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
203.0.113.9 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:58:20 +0000] "GET /login HTTP/1.1" 500 1234 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
203.0.113.9 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:58:25 +0000] "GET /login HTTP/1.1" 500 1234 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
203.0.113.9 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:58:30 +0000] "GET /purchase HTTP/1.1" 500 1456 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
198.51.100.13 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:58:40 +0000] "GET /admin HTTP/1.1" 500 1820 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
203.0.113.9 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:58:50 +0000] "GET /api/v1/users HTTP/1.1" 500 2560 "-" "curl/7.68.0"
203.0.113.25 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:59:01 +0000] "GET /api/v1/users HTTP/1.1" 500 2560 "-" "curl/7.68.0"
203.0.113.9 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:59:10 +0000] "GET /server-error HTTP/1.1" 500 2048 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
192.168.1.10 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:59:30 +0000] "GET /still-missing.html HTTP/1.1" 404 512 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
10.0.0.5 - - [10/Jul/2023:22:59:45 +0000] "GET /lost.html HTTP/1.1" 404 512 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"

3. The reports directory (/home/user/project/reports) does **not** exist initially.

Expected final state after a correct solution:

1. The directory /home/user/project/reports exists (755 or similar).  
2. Inside that directory, exactly the following three files exist, each mode 644:  
   a) 404_summary.log  
   b) 500_top_uris.log  
   c) 301_total.txt

2.a Contents of /home/user/project/reports/404_summary.log

192.168.1.10 3
172.16.0.2 2
10.0.0.5 1

(Ends with a single trailing newline; no extra line breaks.)

2.b Contents of /home/user/project/reports/500_top_uris.log

/server-error 5
/api/v1/users 4
/login 2
/admin 1
/purchase 1

(Exactly five lines, exactly one trailing newline.)

2.c Contents of /home/user/project/reports/301_total.txt

3

(Just the digit 3, newline.)

The grader will check:

• Existence & permissions of /home/user/project/reports.  
• Presence of exactly the three files listed.  
• Byte-for-byte equality of each file’s contents with the strings above.  
• That /home/user/project/logs/access.log remains unchanged.
